title: Reggae on the Rooftop Festival — Juneteenth Weekend
location: AIR Rooftop (formerly Suite Lounge), 75 Luckie St NW, Atlanta, GA
description: Six-hour rooftop reggae/dancehall/Soca/Afrobeats festival with skyline views, complimentary jerk chicken and rum punch (while supplies last). Split a drink, find shade, vibe out — the easiest 'show up and have fun' date on the calendar.

title: AFRO CARIBBEAN FEST Rooftop Party (Free til 11 PM)
location: Cafe Circa, 464 Edgewood Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: Late-night Afrobeats, dancehall, reggae and Soca on a rooftop with international DJs and Atlanta skyline views. Zero-cost entry makes this the no-brainer 'late date' pick — show up after dinner and dance until the lights come up.

title: Happy People Atlanta (Culture & Vibes Festival)
location: Underground Atlanta, 50 Upper Alabama St SW, Atlanta, GA
description: All-day Afrocentric culture festival with music, vendors and food. Built for wandering together and easy to leave whenever you've had enough — great for a first date where you want options.

title: Salsa at Politan Row — Bachata & Social
location: Politan Row at Colony Square, 1197 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Salsa and bachata lessons followed by open social dancing in a food-hall courtyard. Grab tacos and a margarita, then dance — one-stop Sunday afternoon date with built-in dinner.

title: Free Salsa & Bachata Lessons at El Super Pan
location: El Super Pan @ The Battery Atlanta, 800 Battery Ave SE
description: Complimentary group lessons in salsa and bachata at the Latin kitchen inside The Battery. Combine with a Braves game or stroll the Battery afterwards for an easy Sunday date.

title: Vegan Soul Sunday — Real Men Cook Vegan (Father's Day Edition)
location: New Life Presbyterian Church, Atlanta, GA
description: Plant-based soul-food tasting event with Father�s Day energy. Perfect for veggie-curious couples or anyone who likes to eat their way through a date — leave full and happy.

title: Plant to the Future: Food, Freedom & Black Futures Conference
location: Pittsburgh Yards / The Food Studio, Atlanta, GA
description: Plant-based food conference with cooking demos, panels and tastings all day. A date for couples who like learning something together and leaving with new dinner ideas.

title: R.A.S World Cup Atlanta — 100% Reggae, Afrobeat, Soca
location: Air Rooftop Bar Atl, downtown Atlanta
description: World Cup-themed Caribbean rooftop party — three rooms, DJs, dancing til late. Great excuse to dress up and go big for a date night that's more 'event' than 'dinner'.

title: A Taste of Luxury by Chef Brittany
description: Multi-course chef tasting menu — intimate setting built for couples. Small-batch, reservation-style experience that feels like a private dinner date.

title: Best of Atlanta Comedy Showcase
location: Laughing Skull Lounge, 878 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Rotating lineup of Atlanta's best local comics in a 70-seat room — short sets mean nonstop laughs and easy conversation on the walk home. Classic mid-week date night move.

title: Mo Amer at Helium Comedy Club
description: Mo Amer (Netflix/Mo) brings his story-driven stand-up to Atlanta. Big-name headliner in a proper comedy-club setting — easy 'dinner nearby, then show' date.

title: Get Up & Dance Latin Social
location: Atlanta (Salsa Vida studio area) — see listing
description: Beginner-friendly salsa lesson followed by a social floor — no partner needed, all ages welcome. The lowest-cost way to dance together this month.

title: Vinyl Sessions: June Edition
description: Curated listening party of full album sides on vinyl — paired with food and cocktails. A mellow, conversation-friendly date for couples who care about music.

title: T. Murph at City Winery Atlanta
location: City Winery Atlanta, 650 North Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Viral stand-up T. Murph takes on parenting, marriage and everyday life — perfect for couples who want to laugh at themselves. Dinner-and-a-show format means one ticket covers the whole date.

title: Dinner and A Date: A Night of Soul Live Music
description: Live soul/R&B music with dinner built into the experience — the date is literally in the title. Easy pick for couples who don't want to plan logistics.

title: Jokes & Cheese Clean Comedy Show
description: Clean, comedy-and-cheese tasting pairing — crowd-pleaser format that's safe for a first date or double-date. Pair laughs with bites.

title: GAZE — Black LGBTQ+ Artists Group Show
time: Gallery hours — see The Bakery Atlanta
description: Group exhibition of 11 Black queer artists curated by Jasmin Ware. Quiet, free, art-date classic — pair with lunch in the surrounding neighborhood.

title: This Is Atlanta — Landmark Exhibition (VINSONart)
description: Group show celebrating Atlanta and the American South. Slow, walkable, conversation-friendly date — easy to pair with dinner in the same neighborhood.

title: From Afrique, With Love — Artemus Jenkins Photo Exhibition
description: Atlanta photographer Artemus Jenkins' exhibition — a romantic, love-themed show perfect for couples. Pair with a Westside Provisions dinner date.
